title: Angela Rayner warns against 'un-British' immigration reforms
description: Most migrant workers will have to wait longer to qualify for permanent residence under the government's proposals.
content:
Angela Rayner has warned government proposals to make it harder for migrants already in the UK to settle permanently are "un-British" and a "breach of trust".
Ministers want to double the time it takes most migrant workers to qualify for permanent re... [2484 chars]

title: Nigeria's president to begin first state visit in 37 years
description: King Charles and senior royals will provide a ceremonial welcome for Nigeria's President Tinubu on his state visit.
content:
King Charles will host the first Nigerian state visit to the UK for 37 years, with President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and his wife Oluremi Tinubu to be given a ceremonial welcome at Windsor Castle.
At the start of the two-day visit, the leaders of Africa's ... [2334 chars]

title: Rayner: government ‘cannot just go through the motions in the face of decline’
description: Former deputy prime minister says Labour’s immigration changes are un-British and Starmer must respond to fall in party’s popularity
content:
Angela Rayner has said the very survival of the Labour party is at stake and warned Keir Starmer that he “cannot go through the motions” in the face of declining support.
In a speech at campaign group Mainstream’s spring reception, the former deputy ... [2426 chars]

title: Trump says Nato making 'foolish mistake' over Iran
description: The US president expresses frustration that US allies are not helping secure the Strait of Hormuz.
content:
President Donald Trump has accused Nato members of making a "foolish mistake" by refusing to help the US secure the Strait of Hormuz during the war with Iran.
Despite the pressure on oil prices brought about by severe disruption to the critical water... [3871 chars]

title: Blackpool, Ipswich, Milton Keynes among nine finalists for UK City of Culture 2029
description: The nine-strong longlist of cities will receive a £60,000 boost of funding to develop a full bid plan.
content:
Blackpool, Inverness-Highland, Ipswich, Middlesbrough and Milton Keynes are among the places in the running for the title of the UK's City of Culture 2029.
